sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) i.q. עֵדָה No. 3, a precept (of God), most frequently in pl. עֶדְֹות (edwoth) inflected in the Aramean manner (like מַלְכוּ, pl. מַלְכְוָן). Psa 119:14, 36 Psa 119:36, 99 Psa 119:99 Neh 9:34.

(2) law, i.q. תֹּורָה, especially used of the decalogue, Exo 25:21 16:34 2Ki 11:12. אֲרֹון הָעֵדוּת the ark of the law, Exo 25:22. אֹהֶל הָעֵדוּת the tent of the law, Num 9:15 17:23 18:2 [of witness, Eng. Vers. see Act 7:44 ]. Used also of the holy rites, Psa 122:4.

(3) revelation, hence a revealed psalm, Psa 60:1 80:1 inasmuch as the authors of the Psalms considered them as revealed: [as of course all Scripture is; the Psalms are quoted with the words, “the Holy Ghost saith,” Heb 3:7 ], ( Psa 40:7 60:8 62:12 81:7 ). Others consider it to mean a lyric poem, one to be sung to the lyre, as if from עֵד i.q. Arab. عُورُ lyre.
